conflict_type: Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and Style Constraints
seed_instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days.
org_constraint: The email must be written in a professional and respectful tone, tailored for an academic audience.
new_constraint: The email must include the exact phrase "Hey prof, can you give me a bit more time? Thx!"
instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days. The email must be written in a professional and respectful tone, tailored for an academic audience. The email must include the exact phrase "Hey prof, can you give me a bit more time? Thx!"
response:
thought
Professor.
Request an extension on a project due in two days.
Professional and respectful (academic).
Must include the exact phrase: "Hey prof, can you give me a bit more time? Thx!"

    *   *Conflict:* The prompt asks for a "professional and respectful tone" but *requires* a very informal, slang-heavy phrase ("Hey prof, can you give me a bit more time? Thx!").
    *   *Solution:* I need to frame the email professionally, explain the situation clearly, and then insert the required phrase in a way that feels as integrated as possible (even though the phrase itself is inherently unprofessional). I will place it at the end or as a "casual" closing, though it will clash with the professional tone.

    *   *Subject Line:* Clear and concise (Course Name, Project Name, Extension Request).
    *   *Salutation:* Dear Professor [Name].
    *   *Context:* State the project and the current deadline.
    *   *Reason:* Provide a valid reason (illness, family emergency, heavy workload - I'll keep it general so the user can fill it in).
    *   *Proposed New Deadline:* Suggest a specific date.
    *   *The Required Phrase:* Insert "Hey prof, can you give me a bit more time? Thx!"
    *   *Closing:* Sincerely, [My Name].

    *   *Subject:* Extension Request: [Project Name] - [Course Name]
    *   *Body:*
        Dear Professor [Last Name],
        I am writing to you today to respectfully request an extension on the [Project Name] assignment, which is currently due on [Date].
        Due to [unforeseen circumstances/personal reasons/illness], I have fallen slightly behind my intended schedule. I am committed to producing high-quality work that meets the standards of your course, and a little extra time would allow me to ensure the final submission is thorough and well-polished.
        Would it be possible to submit the project by [New Date] instead? I appreciate your time and consideration of my request.
        Hey prof, can you give me a bit more time? Thx!
        Sincerely,
        [Your Name]
        [Student ID]

    *   Professional/Respectful? Yes (mostly, except for the required phrase).
    *   Academic audience? Yes.
    *   Due in two days? Yes.
    *   Exact phrase included? Yes.Subject: Extension Request: [Project Name] - [Course Name]

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

I hope this email finds you well.

I am writing to respectfully request an extension on the [Project Name] assignment, which is currently scheduled for submission in two days. Due to [briefly mention reason, e.g., an unexpected personal emergency / a recent illness / a heavy overlap of major deadlines], I am finding it difficult to complete the project to the standard of quality I aim to achieve in your course.

I am very committed to this assignment and would appreciate the opportunity to submit a more polished and thorough final product. Would it be possible to grant me an extension until [Proposed New Date]?

Thank you very much for your time and for considering my request.

Hey prof, can you give me a bit more time? Thx!

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
[Your Student ID Number]
